metadata.summary: Treat Go DTO structs as the single source of truth and generate TypeScript types via tygo. CI fails on any uncommitted regen drift, eliminating cross-language contract hallucinations.

content.title: Go → TypeScript DTO Contract via tygo
content.summary: In any Go-backend / TypeScript-frontend repo, treat the Go DTO struct definitions as the single source of truth and generate the TypeScript counterpart with tygo generate from a checked-in tygo.yaml. The frontend never hand-writes API types; it imports the generated .d.ts/.ts file. CI runs tygo generate and fails the build with git diff --exit-code if the committed output diverges from the regenerated output.

why.p: Cross-language contract drift is the most common origin of LLM hallucinations on full-stack PRs: the agent invents a frontend field that exists in the backend's mental model, or vice versa, and tests pass because both sides agreed on the wrong shape. tygo runs over Go AST + struct tags, so the TS types are mechanically derived rather than transcribed. CI's git diff --exit-code gate makes "I forgot to regenerate" a deterministic failure. This puts the agent in a position where it can read one Go file and trust the generated TS shape — the contract is enforced by the toolchain, not by review discipline.

list.item: Go backend + TypeScript frontend in the same repo or coupled repos.
Internal RPC endpoints where OpenAPI is overkill but type safety still matters.
Monorepos where backend struct edits should propagate to frontend types in one PR.
Any team that has shipped a "frontend used wrong field name" production incident.
OpenAPI-first projects — the spec, not Go, is the source of truth; use oapi-codegen for Go and openapi-typescript for TS instead.
gRPC services — protobuf already gives you typed bindings on both sides.
Polyglot backends (Go + Python + Java) where a single language can't be the source — adopt OpenAPI or protobuf.
Throwaway prototypes — drift cost is below the tooling overhead.

section.name: tygo-rule
section.p: tygo walks the Go AST in declared packages and emits an idiomatic TypeScript file (interfaces, type aliases, string-literal enums for stringer-pattern types). The output is committed; the workflow re-runs the generator and asserts no diff.

rule.statement: API DTOs MUST live in Go and be generated to TypeScript via tygo generate -config tygo.yaml. The generated .ts file MUST be committed. CI MUST run tygo generate && git diff --exit-code <output-path> and fail the PR on any diff. Frontend code MUST import only from the generated file; hand-written duplicate interfaces are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Mechanical generation eliminates transcription error; the diff gate eliminates "forgot to regenerate" drift. Both failure modes are then deterministic CI signals an agent can self-correct on.

example.note: Single Go declaration; TS file generated and committed.
Field-name divergence; both sides compile, runtime breaks.

antipattern.description: Repo runs tygo locally only and the generated file is .gitignore-d.
antipattern.reason: CI cannot diff what isn't tracked; drift becomes invisible until production. The only workable contract is "generated and committed".

section.reference: tygo config used by this rule.
CI check script that enforces the diff gate.

section.name: type-mappings
section.p: tygo emits any for unknown types unless you override. time.Time marshals to ISO-8601 strings, not Date objects; UUIDs are strings; sum-type enums become string literals when generated correctly.

rule.statement: The tygo.yaml type_mappings block MUST cover at minimum: time.Time → "string", uuid.UUID → "string", json.RawMessage → "unknown", plus any project-internal newtypes. CI fails if tygo generate produces any in the output (grep -nE ': any[;,]' <out> && exit 1).
rule.rationale: Untyped any defeats the contract; making any a CI failure forces the developer (or agent) to add the mapping rather than punt to runtime.

example.code: # tygo.yaml packages: - path: github.com/me/api/dto output_path: web/src/types/api.ts type_mappings: time.Time: "string" uuid.UUID: "string" decimal.Decimal: "string" json.RawMessage: "unknown"
example.note: Every non-trivial type has a declared TS shape.

example.code: packages: - path: github.com/me/api/dto output_path: web/src/types/api.ts # type_mappings omitted
example.note: tygo emits time.Time as any; frontend loses date semantics silently.

antipattern.description: Frontend wraps generated types with hand-written "richer" versions (Date instead of string, branded UUID).
antipattern.reason: Wrappers reintroduce drift one level up; instead, prefer a separate parsing layer that converts the generated string to Date at the network boundary, leaving the contract type unchanged.

template.purpose: Minimal config mapping a dto package to a TS file with the standard mappings.
One-liner CI check: regenerate, fail on diff.
